# The Calibrated View
> A trilogy about perception, presence, and leadership by Marcus Corvin.

## About
The Calibrated View is a three-volume nonfiction trilogy published under Creative Commons (CC BY-NC-ND 4.0). It teaches a practice: see clearly, stay present, fill what only you can see. Foundation, method, mastery.

## Author
Marcus Corvin (pen name). He does not believe in heroes. He believes in rooms — and in the ordinary people who fill them.

## Volumes

### Volume I: The First Lens — See What Is Actually There
36 chapters across four seasons (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter). Covers nine perceptual lenses: family, cultural, trauma, confirmation, authority, information, identity, group, and temporal. Each chapter uses real stories — from Fannie Lou Hamer to John Bowlby to Che Guevara — to reveal how inherited filters shape what you see. Approximately 42,000 words.
- Download: https://marcus-corvin.github.io/thecalibratedview/the-calibrated-view-vol1.pdf

### Volume II: The Full Room — Stay Through What Matters
32 chapters across four seasons. Covers presence, participation, and what it costs to remain in a room when the room gets difficult. Topics include belonging price, manufactured consensus, community practice, and staying through friction. Approximately 28,000 words.
- Download: https://marcus-corvin.github.io/thecalibratedview/the-calibrated-view-vol2.pdf

### Volume III: The Gap — Fill What Only You Can See
28 chapters across four seasons plus a special chapter. Covers leadership as gap-filling — what happens when someone steps forward to do what only they can see needs doing. Stories include Jadav Payeng, Septima Clark, and Wangari Maathai. Key thesis: "The world does not need more leaders. It needs more rooms that work without one." Approximately 33,000 words.
- Download: https://marcus-corvin.github.io/thecalibratedview/the-calibrated-view-vol3.pdf

## Key Concepts
- The nine lenses (perceptual filters inherited from family, culture, trauma, etc.)
- Snapshot thinking vs. process thinking
- The belonging price
- The manufactured view
- The calibrated view
- Sustainability & Empowerment as quality tests: "Can this continue? Can you steer?"
- The gap — the space between what is needed and who is willing to fill it
- See. Stay. Fill. — three verbs, three volumes, one practice

## Framework
Every chapter is measured against two questions:
1. **Can this continue?** (Sustainability) — Is the trajectory self-reinforcing or self-depleting?
2. **Can you steer?** (Empowerment) — Does the reader see more clearly after this, and is that clarity theirs to keep?
